summary: Decompiled seven stage-related ground functions across generic stage animation and five stages: Corneria, Kongo Jungle, Home-Run Contest, Dream Land/Past Stages Pupupu, and Rainbow Cruise. The PR replaced placeholder comments with matching C implementations, tightened several function prototypes, added missing static data/externs, and refined Rainbow Cruise ground variable layout. The PR body states all seven functions were verified 100% matching by an overnight script.
what_changed: Implemented grAnime_801C83D0 as a bool wrapper around grAnime_801C8318 and HSD_AObjGetFlags, returning true when the AObj flags contain 0x40000000.
Added <baselib/aobj.h> include in granime.c to support HSD_AObjGetFlags usage.

Implemented grCorneria_801DDDA8 with typed parameters HSD_GObj* and Vec3*, calculating a Corneria position from the ground object's user_data, a Great Fox/Corneria ground object, JObj world position, and offsets from grCn_803E1D38; it also zeroes the vector on NULL gobj and clamps y to at least 100.0f.
Moved local Corneria data typedefs and extern declarations for grCn_Entry and grCn_Data earlier in grcorneria.c so grCorneria_801DDDA8 can use them.
Updated grcorneria.h prototype for grCorneria_801DDDA8 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void grCorneria_801DDDA8(HSD_GObj*, Vec3*).

Implemented grHomeRun_8021EDD4, computing language-dependent scale/max distance values, tick count, and per-tick ratio globals for Home-Run Contest setup using lbLang_IsSavedLanguageJP and Ground_801C0498.
Declared grHr_804D6AD8 as an f32 global in grhomerun.c alongside existing Home-Run Contest globals.

Implemented static fn_801D7700 in grkongo.c as a collision callback that inspects CollData flags, checks mpLib_GroundEnum_Unk1, samples map vertices with mpVtxGetPos, bins the collision x-position into 15 segments, clamps the index, and increments grKg_803E188C[idx].unk10 by a radian-scaled value from grKg_804D6980->unkA4.

Implemented grKongo_801D7F78 to compare the current Kongo object position against other ground/entity positions from HSD_GObj_Entities->x14 via grKongo_801D7E78, returning true when another object is within the specific z range (-150, -100) and x-distance threshold dx*dx < 6400.0f.
Updated grkongo.h prototype for grKongo_801D7E78 from unknown parameters to Vec3* grKongo_801D7E78(HSD_GObj*, Vec3*).
Extended the static Kongo data struct in grkongo.static.h with f32 unk9C, unkA0, and unkA4 so the new callback can access unkA4.
Implemented grOldPupupu_80210A24 to trigger grAnime_801C8138 based on gp->gv.unk.xC8, xCC, and xD4 when grOp_804D6A9C is zero and xD0 is set.
Added three small static animation index tables in groldpupupu.c: grOp_804D48A0, grOp_804D48A8, and grOp_804D48B0.

Implemented grRCruise_80200074 to initialize a Rainbow Cruise ground object: setup JObj/map animation, start animation for three joints from grRc_804D4790, store mp joint IDs and JObj pointers, install mpJointSetCb1 callback fn_80200460, and zero per-entry state fields.
Added extern s16 grRc_804D4790[4] in grrcruise.c.
Added struct grRCruise_SubEntry to types.h and replaced grRCruise_GroundVars pad_3C[0x30] with typed x3C[3] entries containing state bytes/ints, joint id, and HSD_JObj pointer.
smart_moves: Kept newly decompiled functions narrowly scoped and matched existing stage code style, replacing only placeholder markers while avoiding broad refactors.
Used header prototype tightening as part of the decomp work, converting unknown signatures to typed signatures where callsites and implementations justified them.
Converted Rainbow Cruise's opaque 0x30-byte padding into a 3-element typed subentry array exactly matching the new function's indexed access pattern.
For Kongo collision math, preserved likely compiler-sensitive structure by using nested blocks, duplicated idx/idx2 casts, explicit clamp branches, and the literal 0.017453292F radian conversion.
For Corneria, retained pad/offset-based access into grCn_Data rather than over-claiming a fully known structure, which keeps the match grounded while still documenting the observed layout.
Used language-specific constants in Home-Run Contest setup, preserving Japanese versus non-Japanese behavior with lbLang_IsSavedLanguageJP.
decomp_lessons: Small stage functions often require improving adjacent type information first: globals, extern arrays, static tables, and ground variable substructures may need to be introduced before the function can be expressed cleanly.
When only part of a large data blob is understood, a hybrid struct with explicit padding and typed slices can be preferable to inventing a complete layout.
Ground object callbacks frequently combine Ground user_data, map IDs, JObjs, mpLib joint IDs, and stage-specific gv fields; matching implementations should follow those existing access conventions.
Header updates should accompany decompiled functions once a stable signature is known, especially replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete HSD_GObj, Ground_GObj, Vec3, and bool signatures.
For matching arithmetic, preserve float literal spelling and operation order, especially around casts from float to int, clamp bounds, and repeated calls such as Ground_801C0498.
Stage animation completion checks can be implemented as thin wrappers around shared animation helpers plus HSD AObj flags, avoiding duplicate animation traversal logic.
Opaque ground variable padding can be safely refined when a function demonstrates a repeated array stride and fields at stable offsets.
naming_conventions: Stage-specific symbols keep the established gr prefix plus stage abbreviation, such as grCn_ for Corneria, grKg_ for Kongo, grHr_ for Home-Run, grOp_ for Old Pupupu, and grRc_ for Rainbow Cruise.
Unknown or partially understood fields retain offset-style names such as xC8, xCC, xD0, xD4, unk9C, unkA0, and unkA4 instead of speculative semantic names.
New helper structs use stage-prefixed names when local to a stage, e.g. grCn_Entry and grCn_Data, and descriptive but still conservative names for generic layout refinements, e.g. grRCruise_SubEntry.
Callback-like local functions may retain fn_address naming, as with static fn_801D7700, when no better semantic name is established.
Function declarations continue using address-bearing names such as grHomeRun_8021EDD4 and grRCruise_80200074 until semantic renaming is justified elsewhere.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in functions where stack layout needed padding for matching, including grCorneria_801DDDA8 and grKongo_801D7F78.
Used explicit casts through int and f32 pointer access into padded data in grCorneria_801DDDA8 to match layout-sensitive loads.
Kept branch structure simple and close to assembly, such as grAnime_801C83D0 returning true inside the flag check and false afterward rather than collapsing directly to a boolean expression.
Used repeated local variables idx and idx2 in fn_801D7700 to preserve cast/clamp behavior for the segment index.
Iterated HSD_GObj_Entities->x14 manually in grKongo_801D7F78, skipping the source object and continuing on NULL position results before applying distance tests.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body reports that all seven functions were 100% matching and verified by an overnight script.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type grCn_Data and grCn_Entry in Corneria.
Search for additional users of grAnime_801C83D0 and HSD_AObjGetFlags 0x40000000 to confirm stage animation completion semantics.
Inspect later Kongo Jungle PRs for semantic names for grKg_804D6980->unkA4 and grKg_803E188C[].unk10.
Look for subsequent refinements of grRCruise_SubEntry fields x00, x02, x04, x08, and x0C.
Search Home-Run Contest code around grHr_804D6AD8, grHr_804D6ADC, grHr_804D6AE0, and grHr_804D6AE4 for better variable names.
